title: Rog Nidana & Vikruti Vigyana Proforma
subtitle: NCISM — Pathology & Ayurveda Diagnosis (Nidana Panchaka + Samprapti)

fields.content: 🌿 Nidana Panchaka is the 5-pillar Ayurveda diagnostic tool: (1) Nidana (cause), (2) Purvarupa (premonitory signs), (3) Rupa (clinical features), (4) Upashaya (relieving factors), (5) Samprapti (pathogenesis). Documenting all 5 allows complete Samprapti Vighatana (treatment targeting).

fields.label: Nidana (Causative factors)
fields.placeholder: Aaharaja: Viruddha, Guru, Amla, Ushna excess. Viharaja: Divaswapna, Vegadharana, overexertion. Manasika: Chinta, Shoka, Bhaya, Krodha, Lobha. Agantuja: trauma, infection, toxin. Swabhavaja: constitutional/hereditary…
fields.hint: Always document Vyanjaka Hetu (precipitating cause) separately — what triggered this episode?

fields.label: Purvarupa (Premonitory symptoms — before disease manifests)
fields.placeholder: Early warning signs appearing weeks/months before main disease. e.g. Timira (blurring) before Linganasha; Udgara (belching) before Amlapitta; Gaurava (heaviness) before Ama fever…
fields.hint: Purvarupa = Stage 4 Sthanasamshraya in Kriyakala — treating here can prevent full disease

fields.label: Rupa (Clinical features — Vyanjaka Lakshana)
fields.placeholder: Specific symptoms characteristic of this disease. Include: Pratyatma Lakshana (pathognomonic sign unique to this disease) + Samanya Lakshana (general signs). e.g. Shwasa (breathlessness) + Kapha Samshlishta Srotas (sticky channels) + Sadanashraya (heaviness) in Tamaka Shwasa…

fields.label: Upashaya (Relieving factors — Diagnostic clue)
fields.placeholder: Ahara/Vihara/Aushadha that relieves symptoms. e.g. Hot food + dry conditions relieves Kaphaja; cold/cooling relieves Pittaja; oily/warm relieves Vataja. Upashaya = confirms Dosha when opposite relieves.
fields.hint: Upashaya by Vipareeta (opposites): warming relieves Vata; cooling relieves Pitta; drying relieves Kapha

fields.label: Anupashaya (Aggravating factors)
fields.placeholder: What worsens the condition. e.g. Cold, dry foods worsen Vataja; hot, spicy worsen Pittaja; oily, sweet, heavy worsen Kaphaja. Anupashaya = same quality as Dosha involved.

fields.content: 🌿 Samprapti = complete pathological chain. Samprapti Vighatana (disrupting the chain) is the core of Ayurveda chikitsa. Identify which Ghataka to target: if Agni is poor → Deepana first; if Ama present → Pachana; if Srotas obstructed → Shodhana.

fields.label: Samprapti narrative (step-by-step chain)
fields.placeholder: E.g.: Mandagni (weak digestion) → Ama formation → Vata aggravation → Ama+Vata accumulate in Pakwashaya → spread via Rasavaha Srotas → Sthana Samshraya in Hridaya → Vatika Hridroga manifestation…

fields.label: Kriyakala stage
fields.hint: Best time to treat: Stages 2–3 (Purvarupa stage). Stage 6 = Krichrasadhya/Asadhya

fields.label: Pathological mechanism (modern)
fields.placeholder: e.g. Type 2 hypersensitivity (autoimmune); chronic inflammation → fibrosis; insulin resistance → hyperglycaemia

fields.label: Investigation plan (Pariksha)
fields.placeholder: Primary screen (CBC, LFT, RFT, lipids, urine). Specific (HbA1c, ANA, ANCA, tumour markers). Imaging (USG, X-ray, CT/MRI). Histopathology (biopsy, FNAC, bone marrow). Monitoring parameters (follow-up interval + tests)…

fields.label: Gold-standard confirmatory test
fields.placeholder: e.g. Bone marrow biopsy (Leukaemia), Endoscopy + biopsy (IBD), HbA1c ≥6.5% (DM)

fields.label: Classical text reference
fields.placeholder: e.g. Charaka Chikitsa 16/5; Madhava Nidana 22/4; Sushruta Nidana 7/3

fields.label: Ayurveda differential (Vyadhi Visheshana)
fields.placeholder: Dosha-specific differentiation: e.g. Vataja Gulma vs Pittaja Gulma vs Kaphaja Gulma — distinguishing features, key Nadi/Rupa differences…
